title: celtis sinensis f. purpurascens
intro: Celtis sinensis f. purpurascens, also known as the Purple Hackberry or Purpurascens, is a cultivar of the Celtis sinensis tree native to China. It is primarily valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, and is said to have potential health benefits such as lowering cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health. Celtis sinensis f. purpurascens is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
